docs.title: Presentations.AI Developer Documentation
docs.text:
Welcome to Presentations.AI's comprehensive developer documentation. Our platform provides powerful AI-driven tools for creating professional presentations through both REST API and MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration.

🎯 What You'll Find Here

Complete guides for integrating Presentations.AI into your applications, from simple API calls to advanced MCP server connections with Claude Desktop.

docs.title: 🚀 Quick Overview
docs.text:
Presentations.AI enables developers to:

  • Generate presentations from topics using advanced AI
  • Create single slides for focused content and quick generation
  • Transform files into presentations (PDFs, documents, spreadsheets)
  • Create custom presentations from structured content
  • Integrate with Claude Desktop via MCP server for conversational presentation creation

docs.title: 🚀 Two Powerful Integration Methods
docs.text:
• 🔗 REST API Integration

    Traditional HTTP API for developers

    • Simple HTTP requests with JSON responses
    • Bearer token authentication
    • Perfect for web apps, mobile apps, and backend services
    • Get Started with REST API →

  • 🤖 MCP Integration (Recommended)

    Revolutionary Claude Desktop integration for conversational AI experiences

    • Natural language presentation creation
    • Seamless Claude Desktop workflow
    • Real-time collaboration with AI
    • Perfect for AI-powered productivity
    • Set up MCP Integration →

docs.title: Try MCP Integration
docs.text:
🚀 Fastest Way to Get Started (2 minutes setup)

Skip complex API calls! Create presentations by talking to Claude Desktop:

1. Install Claude Desktop → Download here

2. Add MCP Server → Follow our setup guide

3. Start Creating! 

"Hey Claude, create a 7-slide presentation about digital marketing trends for 2024"

That's it! Claude will generate, edit, and export your presentation automatically.

docs.title: MCP in Action
docs.text:
You: "Create a presentation about AI in healthcare with 8 slides"

Claude: I'll create that presentation for you! Let me generate 8 slides about AI in healthcare.

✅ Generated: "AI in Healthcare - Transforming Patient Care.pptx"
📥 Download: https://cdn.presentations.ai/exports/199943/...

Create additional content?

You: "Create same presentation on same topic with 15 slides"

Claude: I'll create a new presentation on the same topic with 15 slides...
✅ Generated: "AI in Healthcare - Transforming Patient Care.pptx" (15 slides)
📥 Download: https://cdn.presentations.ai/exports/199944/...

docs.title: 🔗 Or Use REST API
docs.text: Prefer traditional API integration? Get started with REST API in under 5 minutes:

docs.title: Get Your API Key
docs.text: Register at developers.presentations.ai and get your free API key (200 credits included).

docs.title: 2. Create Your First Presentation
docs.text:
curl -X POST https://developers.presentations.ai/api/v1/topic/document \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -F "topic=Introduction to Machine Learning" \
  -F "slideCount=8" \
  -F "language=en" \
  -F "domain=presentations.ai" \
  -F "exportType=ppt" \
  # Optional: include a file (PDF, Word, PowerPoint, or text)
  -F "file=@/path/to/yourfile.pdf"'

docs.title: 3. Success Response
docs.text:
{
    "status": 0,
    "docid": 199943,
    "docurl": "https://cdn.presentations.ai/exports/199943/1755583984885/Digital%20Marketing%20for%20Small%20Businesses.pptx",
    "animated_url": "https://cdn.presentations.ai/exports/199943/1755583984566/Digital%20Marketing%20for%20Small%20Businesses_animated.pptx"
}

🎉 That's it!

Your AI-generated presentation is ready! Visit the docurl to view, edit, or download your presentation.

docs.title: 📚 Documentation Sections
docs.text:
• 🤖 MCP Integration ⭐ Recommended

    Revolutionary Claude Desktop integration for conversational AI presentation creation

  • Getting Started

    Account registration, API key setup, and authentication

  • REST API Reference

    Complete REST API documentation with examples

docs.title: 🔑 Authentication
docs.text:
All API requests require authentication using your API key as a Bearer token:

Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY

docs.title: Code Examples:
🛟 Need Help?
docs.text:
• Email Support: support@presentations.ai

💡 Pro Tip

Start with our Getting Started guide for a step-by-step walkthrough, or jump directly to the API Reference if you're ready to integrate.

docs.title: 🚨 Error Handling Guide
docs.text:
This comprehensive guide covers all possible errors you might encounter when using the Presentations.AI API, along with solutions and best practices.

📋 Error Response Format

All API errors follow a consistent format with status codes and descriptive messages to help you debug quickly.

docs.title: 📊 Standard Error Response
docs.text:
All API endpoints return errors in this format:

{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Descriptive error message"
}

docs.title: Response Fields:
docs.text:
• status: Always 1 for errors, 0 for success
  • error: Human-readable error description
  • HTTP Status Code: Standard HTTP status codes (400, 401, 402, 500, etc.)

docs.title: Invalid API Key
docs.text:
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Invalid API key"
}

Causes: - API key is incorrect or malformed - API key has been reset/regenerated - Missing or malformed Authorization header

Solutions: - Verify your API key in the developer portal - Ensure header format: Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY - Generate a new API key if necessary

docs.title: Missing Authorization Header
docs.text:
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Unauthorized access"
}

Solution: 

# ❌ Wrong - Missing header
curl -X POST https://developers.presentations.ai/v1/topic/document

# ✅ Correct - With authorization
curl -X POST https://developers.presentations.ai/v1/topic/document \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY"

docs.title: Insufficient Credits
docs.text:
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Insufficient credits for this operation"
}

Solutions: - Purchase more credits - Contact support for billing issues

docs.title: Account Suspended
docs.text:
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Account suspended. Contact support."
}

Solution: Contact support@presentations.ai for account resolution.

docs.title: Empty or Invalid Topic
docs.text:
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Topic cannot be empty"
}

Solutions: 

// ❌ Wrong - Empty topic
{
  "topic": "",
  "exportType": "ppt"
}

// ✅ Correct - Descriptive topic
{
  "topic": "Introduction to Machine Learning Algorithms",
  "exportType": "ppt"
}

docs.title: Invalid Slide Count
docs.text:
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Slide count must be between 1 and 50"
}

Valid Range: 1-50 slides

// ❌ Wrong
{
  "topic": "AI Overview",
  "slideCount": 100
}

// ✅ Correct
{
  "topic": "AI Overview", 
  "slideCount": 25
}

docs.title: Invalid Export Type
docs.text:
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Invalid export type. Must be: ppt, pptx, pdf, image, render, or share"
}

Valid Export Types: - ppt - PowerPoint file (.ppt format) - pptx - PowerPoint file (.pptx format) - pdf - PDF document - image - Preview images - render - Rendered presentation - share - Shareable link

docs.title: Internal Server Error
docs.text:
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Internal server error. Please try again later."
}

Solutions: - Wait a few minutes and retry - Contact support if persistent

docs.title: 🆘 Getting Help
docs.text:
When contacting support, please include:

  1. Error message (complete JSON response)
  2. Request details (endpoint, method, parameters)
  3. Timestamp of the error
  4. Your API key (last 4 characters only)
  5. Registered email address associated with your API key (the one used during key generation)
  6. Expected behavior vs actual behavior

Contact Information: - Email: support@presentations.ai

💡 Pro Tips

  • Always validate input before sending requests
  • Implement proper error handling and retries for server errors
  • Cache successful responses when possible
  • Monitor your credit usage proactively

docs.title: ⚡ Quick Start Guide
docs.text:
Get up and running with Presentations.AI in under 10 minutes! This guide will walk you through creating your first AI-generated presentation.

🎯 What You'll Learn

  • Account setup and API key generation
  • Your first API call
  • Understanding responses and next steps
  • Testing with different programming languages

docs.title: 🚀 Step 1: Create Your Account
docs.text:
1. Visit https://developers.presentations.ai/docs/login
  2. Sign up using:
  3. LinkedIn, Google, or Microsoft (instant access)
  4. Email (requires email confirmation)
  5. Complete the registration process

✅ Free Credits

New accounts receive 200 free credits to get started!

docs.title: 🔑 Step 2: Get Your API Key
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Settings → API / Register for API
  2. Click "Register for API" button
  3. Click "Reveal" to display your API key
  4. Copy and store your API key securely

🔒 Security Note

Never commit API keys to version control or share them publicly. Store them as environment variables or in secure configuration.

docs.title: 🎯 Step 3: Make Your First API Call
docs.text: Let's create a simple presentation about "AI in Healthcare":

docs.title: Using cURL:
docs.text:
curl -X POST https://developers.presentations.ai/api/v1/topic/document \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY_HERE" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "topic": "AI in Healthcare: Transforming Patient Care",
    "slideCount": 6,
    "language": "en", 
    "domain": "presentations.ai",
    "exportType": "ppt"
  }'

docs.title: Using JavaScript (Node.js):
docs.text:
const axios = require('axios');

const createPresentation = async () => {
  try {
    const response = await axios.post('https://developers.presentations.ai/api/v1/topic/document', {
      topic: "AI in Healthcare: Transforming Patient Care",
      slideCount: 6,
      language: "en",
      domain: "healthcare", 
      exportType: "ppt"
    }, {
      headers: {
        'Authorization': 'Bearer YOUR_API_KEY_HERE',
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
      }
    });

    console.log('Success:', response.data);
    return response.data;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error:', error.response.data);
  }
};

createPresentation();

docs.title: Using Python:
docs.text:
import requests
import json

def create_presentation():
    url = "https://developers.presentations.ai/api/v1/topic/document"

    headers = {
        "Authorization": "Bearer YOUR_API_KEY_HERE",
        "Content-Type": "application/json"
    }

    data = {
        "topic": "AI in Healthcare: Transforming Patient Care",
        "slideCount": 6,
        "language": "en",
        "domain": "presentations.ai",
        "exportType": "ppt"
    }

    try:
        response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=data)
        response.raise_for_status()

        result = response.json()
        print("Success:", json.dumps(result, indent=2))
        return result

    except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
        print(f"Error: {e}")

create_presentation()

docs.title: 🎉 Step 4: Understanding the Response
docs.text:
A successful response looks like this:

{
    "status": 0,
    "docid": 199943,
    "docurl": "https://cdn.presentations.ai/exports/199943/1755583984885/Digital%20Marketing%20for%20Small%20Businesses.pptx",
    "animated_url": "https://devcdn.presentations.ai/exports/199943/1755583984566/Digital%20Marketing%20for%20Small%20Businesses_animated.pptx"
}

docs.title: Response Fields:
docs.text:
• status: 0 = success, 1 = error
  • message: Human-readable status message
  • docurl: Direct link to view/edit your presentation
  • insertid: Unique presentation ID for future operations

🎊 Congratulations!

Click the docurl to view your AI-generated presentation!

docs.title: 🔧 Step 5: Try Different Export Types
docs.text: Presentations.AI supports multiple export formats:

docs.title: PowerPoint File (.ppt):
docs.text:
{
  "topic": "Your topic here",
  "exportType": "ppt"
}

docs.title: PowerPoint File (.pptx):
docs.text:
{
  "topic": "Your topic here",
  "exportType": "pptx"
}

docs.title: PDF Document:
docs.text:
{
  "topic": "Your topic here", 
  "exportType": "pdf"
}

docs.title: Web Shareable Link:
docs.text:
{
  "topic": "Your topic here",
  "exportType": "share"
}

docs.title: Preview Images:
docs.text:
{
  "topic": "Your topic here",
  "exportType": "image"
}

docs.title: 🌍 Step 6: Explore Language Support
docs.text:
Create presentations in multiple languages:

{
  "topic": "Inteligencia Artificial en la Medicina",
  "language": "es",
  "domain": "presentations.ai"
}

Supported Languages: - en - English - es - Spanish - fr - French - de - German - pt - Portuguese - it - Italian - And many more...

docs.title: 🎯 Step 7: Customize by Domain
docs.text:
Get better results by specifying the domain context:

BusinessEducationTechnologyHealthcare 
{
  "topic": "Digital Marketing Strategy 2024",
  "domain": "yourdomain.com",
  "slideCount": 12
}

{
  "topic": "Introduction to Quantum Physics", 
  "domain": "education",
  "slideCount": 15
}

{
  "topic": "Cloud Computing Fundamentals",
  "domain": "presentations.ai", 
  "slideCount": 10
}

{
  "topic": "Patient Safety Protocols",
  "domain": "presentations.ai",
  "slideCount": 8
}

docs.title: Authentication Error (401):
docs.text:
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Unauthorized access"
}
Solution: Check your API key and Authorization header format.

docs.title: Insufficient Credits (402):
docs.text:
{
  "status": 1, 
  "error": "Insufficient credits"
}
Solution: Purchase more credits or check your balance.

docs.title: Invalid Topic (400):
docs.text:
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Topic cannot be empty"
}
Solution: Ensure your topic is descriptive and not empty.

docs.title: 🚀 Next Steps
docs.text:
Congratulations! You've successfully created your first AI presentation. Here's what to explore next:

  1. API Reference - Explore all available endpoints
  2. MCP Integration - Connect with Claude Desktop
  3. Best Practices - Tips for optimal results

💡 Pro Tips

  • Use specific, descriptive topics for better results
  • Start with smaller slide counts (5-10) to test
  • Specify domain context for more relevant content
  • Save your insertid for future slide updates

Ready to build something amazing? 🎨

Explore Full API Documentation Get Support

docs.title: 📊 Credits API
docs.text: APIs related to GPT credit pricing, usage history, and availability for different workspaces. All endpoints are prefixed with /credits.

docs.title: 1. GET /credits/getApiCredits
docs.text: Fetch pricing plans for GPT usage for a specific workspace, considering user’s IP location.

docs.title: 🔐 Authorization
docs.text: Requires user to be authenticated (req.session.pd.user).

docs.title: 🧾 Query Parameters
2. GET /credits/getWorkspaceGpt3History
docs.text: Fetch usage history of GPT-3 credits for a given workspace with optional pagination.

docs.title: 🔐 Authorization
docs.text: Requires user to be authenticated (req.session.pd.user).

docs.title: 🧾 Query Parameters
3. GET /credits/getCredits
docs.text: Get pricing plans for GPT usage based on user’s geolocation via IP.

docs.title: 🔐 Authorization
docs.text:
Requires user to be authenticated (req.session.pd.user).

  • Success Response

    {
"status": 0,
"plans": [
    {
    "id": 1,
    "name": "Starter Plan",
    "currency": "USD",
    "credit": "50",
    "symbol": "$",
    "unformattedPrice": 5,
    "price": "5.00"
    },
...
]
}

  • Failure 

    {
  "status": 1,
  "msg": "An error occurred while processing your request"
}

docs.title: 4. GET /credits/getWorkspaceGpt3Details
docs.text: Get the remaining GPT-3 credits for a specific workspace.

docs.title: 🔐 Authorization
docs.text: Requires user to be authenticated (req.session.pd.user).

docs.title: 🧾 Query Parameters
🤖 MCP Integration: Create Presentations with Natural Language
docs.text:
🌟 Revolutionary Presentation Creation

Transform the way you create presentations! Instead of complex API calls, simply chat with Claude Desktop to generate professional presentations instantly.

"Create a 10-slide presentation about renewable energy for executives" → ✨ Done in seconds!

This guide shows you how to connect the Presentations.AI MCP server to Claude Desktop, enabling conversational presentation creation directly in your chats.

docs.title: 🚀 Why Choose MCP Integration?
docs.text:
• 🗣️ Natural Language

    No coding required - just describe what you want

  • 🔄 Interactive Editing

    "Make slide 3 more technical" - Claude updates instantly

  • 🎯 AI-Powered

    Claude understands context and creates relevant content

  • 📦 Export Options

    Includes all the export types same as REST APIs

docs.title: 1️⃣ Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Claude Desktop installed (latest version) Download: https://claude.ai/download
  • A hosted Presentations.AI MCP server running at a valid URL (e.g., https://developers.presentations.ai/mcp)
  • An API key from Presentations.AI Obtain it by registering at https://developers.presentations.ai

💡 Ensure Claude Desktop is updated via the "Check for Updates" option in the Claude menu.

docs.title: 2️⃣ Locate Claude Desktop Configuration File
docs.text:
Claude Desktop uses a file named claude_desktop_config.json to configure MCP servers.

  • macOS 

    ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json

  • Windows 

    %APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json

💡 If the file doesn’t exist, create it in the specified directory using a text editor like VS Code or Notepad++.

docs.title: 3️⃣ Add Your Remote MCP Server Configuration
docs.text:
Edit (or create) claude_desktop_config.json and add your Presentations.AI remote MCP server details using the mcp-remote launcher:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "presentationsai": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": [
        "mcp-remote",
        "https://developers.presentations.ai/mcp",
        "--header",
        "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY"
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Explanation:
docs.text:
• mcpServers: A map of named MCP servers (e.g., presentationsai). The name can be customized.
  • command: Uses npx to run the mcp-remote package, which connects to remote MCP servers.
  • args:
  • mcp-remote specifies the remote server connection.
  • The URL (https://developers.presentations.ai/mcp) is the endpoint for the Presentations.AI MCP server (verify the exact URL from Presentations.AI documentation).
  • The API key is passed via the --header flag for authentication.

💡 Replace YOUR_API_KEY with the API key obtained from https://developers.presentations.ai. Ensure the mcp-remote package is available in your environment or managed by your system administrator.

docs.title: 4️⃣ Restart Claude Desktop
docs.text:
After saving changes to claude_desktop_config.json: 1. Quit Claude Desktop completely (ensure it’s not running in the background). 2. Restart the application. 3. Claude will automatically connect to your Presentations.AI remote MCP server.

💡 On Windows, use Task Manager to confirm Claude Desktop is fully closed. On macOS, use Activity Monitor or run pkill Claude in the terminal.

docs.title: 5️⃣ Verify the Integration
docs.text:
To confirm the MCP server is connected: - Open Claude Desktop and start a new conversation. - Click the hammer icon (or tools icon) in the conversation input area to view available MCP tools. - Look for the presentationsai server or its tools (e.g., presentation creation tools). - Alternatively, check the developer console (View → Toggle Developer Tools) for logs like: 

✅ Connected to MCP server: presentationsai
- Test the integration by asking Claude to create a presentation, e.g., “Create a 5-slide presentation on renewable energy using Presentations.AI.”

💡 If the server doesn’t appear, verify the URL is correct and the server is accessible. Check logs in ~/Library/Logs/Claude/mcp.log (macOS) or %APPDATA%\Claude\mcp.log (Windows) for errors.

docs.title: 🔹 Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Issue Possible Cause Fix MCP server not detected Incorrect JSON syntax or file path Validate JSON syntax (use a JSON linter) and confirm file path. Connection refused Wrong or unreachable server URL Verify the URL with Presentations.AI support. Auth errors Invalid or expired API key Regenerate the API key at https://developers.presentations.ai. Tools not showing Claude Desktop not restarted Fully quit and restart Claude Desktop.

💡 For additional debugging, enable debug logging in Claude Desktop settings (Settings → Developer → Enable Debug Logging) and review logs in the Extensions settings panel or mcp.log file.

docs.title: 🔹 Example Usage
docs.text:
Once connected, you can use natural language in Claude Desktop to interact with the Presentations.AI MCP server. Examples: - “Create a 10-slide presentation on digital marketing using Presentations.AI.” - “Generate a PDF presentation about AI trends with 5 slides.”

The server will process these requests using the Presentations.AI API endpoints (POST /v1/topic/document, POST /v1/content/document, or POST /v1/document/slide), and Claude will return the results, such as a download link or confirmation.

docs.title: 🔹 Additional Notes
docs.text:
• Security: Keep your claude_desktop_config.json file and API key secure, as they contain sensitive information. Use your operating system’s secure storage (e.g., Keychain on macOS, Credential Manager on Windows) for API keys if possible.
  • Multiple Servers: You can add multiple MCP servers in the mcpServers object by defining additional entries with unique names.
  • Desktop Extensions (DXT): If Presentations.AI provides a .dxt file for their MCP server, you can install it via Claude Desktop’s Extensions settings for a simpler setup (Settings → Extensions → Install Extension).

For further assistance, contact Presentations.AI support at support@presentations.ai or visit Anthropic’s Help Center for Claude Desktop issues.

docs.title: How to register for an API account
docs.text: To start using our API you need to register with our platform. Following are the steps involved

docs.title: 1. Register for an account
docs.text:
Go to the developer portal at https://developers.presentations.ai/docs/login

You can sign up using your LinkedIn/Google/Microsoft credentials or use your own email to sign up. However you will have to confirm your account when you use your own credentials.

Note: If you have already have signed up on our platform then you can directly login

docs.title: 2. Request for API Key
docs.text: Click on the Register for API button.

docs.title: 3. API Details
docs.text:
After signup, the api key is displayed. Click on the Reveal button to see the key. Use this key as Bearer Token in your API requests. On Sign-up 200 credits are given to you.

Click on the Reset button to generate a new API Key.

docs.title: POST /v1/content/document
docs.text:
🚧 Beta API Notice

The Generate API is currently in beta. Functionality, rate limits, and pricing are subject to change.

Creates a presentation using raw slide content instead of generating from topic.

docs.title: 🔒 Authorization
docs.text: Requires checkWorkspace.

docs.title: 📥 Request Body
Create Presentation from File
docs.text:
🚧 Beta API Notice

The File to Presentation API is currently in beta. Functionality, rate limits, and pricing are subject to change.

This endpoint allows you to create presentations directly from uploaded files. Upload documents, PDFs, spreadsheets, or text files and let AI transform them into professional presentations.

docs.title: POST /api/v1/document/file
docs.text: Creates a presentation from an uploaded file using AI content analysis and generation.

docs.title: Authorization
docs.text: Include your API key in the Authorization header as a Bearer token.

docs.title: Request Parameters
docs.text: Field Type Required Description file file Yes Content file to convert to presentation topic string Optional Override topic (if not provided, extracted from file). You can use this to instruct the AI, for example: "Split content as it is into slides". The API will primarily use the content in the uploaded file to generate slides. slideCount number Optional Number of slides to generate (1-50) language string Optional Language for generation (default: English) domain string Optional Context domain (e.g., education, business) exportType string Yes One of ppt, pptx, pdf, image, render, share tone string Optional Tone of the presentation (e.g., professional, casual, educational). targetAudience string Optional Target audience for the presentation (e.g., students, executives). callback_url string Optional HTTPS URL to receive async callback notification. preservationMode string Optional How to handle the source content. One of enhance (default), preserve, summarize, instruction.

docs.title: Supported File Formats
docs.text:
• Text: .txt
  • Documents: .docx, .odt
  • eBooks: .epub
  • PDFs: .pdf
  • Web: .html, .htm
  • Data: .tsv, .json, .xls, .xlsx

docs.title: File Size Limit
docs.text:
Maximum file size is 1MB. If you upload a file larger than this, you will receive:

{
    "status": 1,
    "error": "File size exceeds maximum limit of 1MB"
}

docs.title: Callback URL Flow
docs.text:
If you provide a valid callback_url, the API will respond immediately with a job ID and queue status:

Immediate Response (with valid callback_url): 

{
    "job_id": "b1ed6035-1872-49d1-b773-34b920e74204",
    "status": "queued"
}

Immediate Response (with invalid callback_url): 

{
    "status": 1,
    "error": "Invalid callback_url. Must be a valid HTTPS URL"
}

When processing is complete, the result (same as the synchronous response below) will be POSTed to your callback URL.

If you do not provide a callback_url, the result will be returned directly in the API response.

docs.title: Preservation Modes (file → deck)
docs.text:
• enhance (default): AI enhances and structures the deck. slideCount validated (1-50).
  • preserve: Keep the original order/structure; slideCount is ignored (no need to send it).
  • summarize: Condense the content; slideCount validated (1-50).
  • instruction: Follow custom instructions; topic is required and used as the instruction text. slideCount validated (1-50).

docs.title: Timeout & Polling (no callback_url)
docs.text:
• If processing takes longer than 5 minutes, the API returns immediately while continuing in the background.
  • You will receive: 
    {
  "status": 1,
  "pollUrl": "https://developers.presentations.ai/api/v1/polljob/<job_id>"
}
  • Poll the job status with GET https://developers.presentations.ai/api/v1/polljob/<job_id> (same Bearer token).
  • In-progress response: 
    {
  "status": 1,
  "message": "Document is being processed"
}
  • Completed response mirrors the success format for your exportType.

docs.title: Example Request (cURL)
docs.text:
curl -X POST https://developers.presentations.ai/api/v1/document/file \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY_HERE" \
  -F "file=@/path/to/your/document.pdf" \
  -F "topic=Annual Report Highlights" \
  -F "slideCount=10" \
  -F "language=en" \
  -F "domain=presentations.ai" \
  -F "exportType=ppt" \
  -F "tone=professional" \
  -F "targetAudience=executives" \
  -F "preservationMode=instruction" \
  -F "callback_url=https://yourdomain.com/api/callback"

docs.title: Response Formats
docs.text:
The response format depends on the exportType parameter:

exportType = ppt/pptx (Editable PowerPoint File) Success (200): 
{
    "status": 0,
    "docid": 199950,
    "docurl": "https://cdn.presentations.ai/exports/199950/1755583984885/Market%20Analysis%20Report.pptx",
    "animated_url": "https://devcdn.presentations.ai/exports/199950/1755583984566/Market%20Analysis%20Report_animated.pptx"
}
Failure (400, 500): 
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Please provide a content file"
}
exportType = pdf (Sharable PDF) Success (200): 
{
    "status": 0,
    "docid": 199951,
    "docurl": "https://cdn.presentations.ai/pdfs/199951/Market%20Analysis%20Report.pdf",
    "animated_url": null
}
Failure: 
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Failed to generate PDF from file"
}
exportType = image (Slide Images) Success (200): 
{
    "status": 0,
    "docid": 199952,
    "docurl": "https://cdn.presentations.ai/pdfs/199952/Market%20Analysis%20Report.zip",
    "animated_url": null
}
Failure: 
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Image generation failed"
}
exportType = render (Rendered Preview) Success (200): 
{
    "status": 0,
    "docid": 199953,
    "docurl": "https://cdn.presentations.ai/docs/edit/199953"
}
Failure: 
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Failed to render presentation"
}
exportType = share (Public Shareable Link) Success (200): 
{
    "status": 0,
    "docid": 199954,
    "docurl": "https://cdn.presentations.ai/view/YJJOFilucT"
}
Failure: 
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Failed to create share link"
}

docs.title: File-Specific Errors
docs.text:
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "File size exceeds maximum limit of 1MB"
}
Solution: Reduce file size or use async processing with callback_url.

{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Please provide a content file"
}
Solution: Ensure the file parameter contains a valid file upload.

{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Unsupported file format"
}
Solution: Use one of the supported file formats listed above.

{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Unable to extract content from file"
}
Solution: Ensure the file contains readable text content and is not corrupted.

docs.title: Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Topic Guidance: Use the topic field to instruct the AI, e.g., "Split content as it is into slides". The file content is the main source for slide generation.
  • Tone & Target Audience: Use tone to set the style (e.g., professional, casual, educational) and targetAudience to tailor content for specific groups (e.g., students, executives).
  • Callback URL: If provided and valid, results will be sent asynchronously to your endpoint. If invalid, you will receive an error message.

For more details about error handling, see our Error Handling Guide.

docs.title: POST /v1/document/slide
docs.text:
🚧 Beta API Notice

The Generate API is currently in beta. Functionality, rate limits, and pricing are subject to change.

Updates slide content for an existing document.

docs.title: 🔒 Authorization
docs.text: Requires checkWorkspace.

docs.title: 📥 Request Body
docs.text:
Field Type Required Description docId number ✅ Document ID to update. slides string (JSON) ✅ JSON array of slide content objects.

Each slide object in the array must include:

Slide Field Type Description action string Type of update (e.g., update). slideContent string The updated slide text. index number Position of the slide.

docs.title: 📤 Response
docs.text:
• Success (200)

    {
"status": 0,
"message": "Slides updated successfully"
}

  • **Failure **

```json

{ "status": 1, "error": "Slide content cannot be empty" }

docs.title: Presentations.AI API Documentation
docs.text:
🚧 Beta API Notice

The Generate API is currently in beta. Functionality, rate limits, and pricing are subject to change.

This document describes the key endpoints of the Presentations.AI API for creating and managing AI-generated presentations. All endpoints require authentication using a Bearer token obtained from https://developers.presentations.ai.

📚 Available Endpoints

  • Create from Topic - Generate multi-slide presentations from topics
  • Create Single Slide - Generate focused single-slide presentations
  • Create from File - Transform uploaded files into presentations

docs.title: POST /api/v1/topic/document
docs.text: Creates a presentation from a given topic using AI.

docs.title: Authorization
docs.text: Include your API key in the Authorization header as a Bearer token.

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Field Type Required Description topic string Yes The main topic of the presentation. slideCount number Optional Number of slides to generate (1-50). language string Optional Language for generation (default: English). domain string Optional Context domain (e.g., education, business). exportType string Yes One of ppt, pptx, pdf, image, render, share. file string Optional File type (.txt, .docx, .odt, .epub, .pdf, .html, .htm, .tsv, .json, .xls, .xlsx). targetAudience string Optional Target audience for the presentation (e.g., students, executives). tone string Optional Tone of the presentation (e.g., professional, casual, educational). callback_url string Optional HTTPS URL to receive async callback notification.

docs.title: Callback URL Flow
docs.text:
If you provide a valid callback_url, the API will respond immediately with a job ID and queue status:

Immediate Response (with valid callback_url): 

{
    "job_id": "b1ed6035-1872-49d1-b773-34b920e74204",
    "status": "queued"
}

Immediate Response (with invalid callback_url): 

{
    "status": 1,
    "error": "Invalid callback_url. Must be a valid HTTPS URL"
}

When processing is complete, the result (same as the synchronous response below) will be POSTed to your callback URL.

If you do not provide a callback_url (synchronous flow):

The response will be returned directly in the API call, depending on exportType:

docs.title: Timeout & Polling (no callback_url)
docs.text:
• If processing takes longer than 5 minutes, the API returns immediately while continuing in the background.
  • You will receive: 
    {
  "status": 1,
  "pollUrl": "https://developers.presentations.ai/api/v1/polljob/<job_id>"
}
  • Poll the job status with GET https://developers.presentations.ai/api/v1/polljob/<job_id> (same Bearer token).
  • In-progress response: 
    {
  "status": 1,
  "message": "Document is being processed"
}
  • Completed response mirrors the success format for your exportType.

docs.title: Response depends on the exportType
docs.text:
Choose the exportType to view its corresponding response format:

exportType = ppt/pptx (Editable PowerPoint File) Success (200): 
{
    "status": 0,
    "docid": 199943,
    "docurl": "https://cdn.presentations.ai/exports/199943/1755583984885/Digital%20Marketing%20for%20Small%20Businesses.pptx",
    "animated_url": "https://devcdn.presentations.ai/exports/199943/1755583984566/Digital%20Marketing%20for%20Small%20Businesses_animated.pptx"
}
Failure (400, 500): 
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Topic cannot be empty"
}
exportType = pdf (Sharable PDF) Success (200): 
{
    "status": 0,
    "docid": 199926,
    "docurl": "https://cdn.presentations.ai/pdfs/199926/Digital%20Marketing%20for%20Small%20Businesses.pdf",
    "animated_url": null
}
Failure: 
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Failed to generate PDF"
}
exportType = image (Slide Images) Success (200): 
{
    "status": 0,
    "docid": 199953,
    "docurl": "https://cdn.presentations.ai/pdfs/199953/Digital%20Marketing%20for%20Small%20Businesses.zip",
    "animated_url": null
}
Failure: 
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Image generation failed"
}
exportType = render (Rendered Preview) Success (200): 
{
    "status": 0,
    "docid": 199944,
    "docurl": "https://cdn.presentations.ai/docs/edit/199944"
}
Failure: 
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Failed to render presentation"
}
exportType = share (Public Shareable Link) Success (200): 
{
    "status": 0,
    "docid": 199945,
    "docurl": "https://cdn.presentations.ai/view/YJJOFilucT"
}
Failure: 
{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Failed to create share link"
}

docs.title: Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Authentication: Obtain your API key from https://developers.presentations.ai and include it as Authorization: Bearer <your-api-key>.
  • Export Types:
    • ppt/pptx → Downloadable PowerPoint
    • pdf → Sharable PDF
    • image → Slide previews in image format
    • render → Rendered preview of the presentation
    • share → Public shareable link
  • Best Practices: Specify domain for better context, use descriptive topics, and test with small slideCount values initially.
  • Target Audience & Tone: Use targetAudience to tailor content for specific groups (e.g., students, executives) and tone to set the style (e.g., professional, casual, educational).
  • Callback URL: If provided and valid, results will be sent asynchronously to your endpoint. If invalid, you will receive an error message.

For more details, visit Presentations.AI or contact support@presentations.ai.

docs.title: Create Single Slide from Topic
docs.text:
🚧 Beta API Notice

The Single Slide API is currently in beta. Functionality, rate limits, and pricing are subject to change.

This endpoint allows you to create a single slide presentation from a given topic using AI. Perfect for quick slide generation or when you need just one slide for your content.

docs.title: POST /api/v1/topic/singleslide
docs.text: Creates a single slide presentation from a given topic using AI.

docs.title: Authorization
docs.text: Include your API key in the Authorization header as a Bearer token.

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text:
Field Type Required Description topic string Yes The main topic for the slide. language string Optional Language for generation (default: English). domain string Optional Context domain (e.g., education, business). file file Optional Additional reference file (.txt, .docx, .odt, .epub, .pdf, .html, .htm, .tsv, .json, .xls, .xlsx). targetAudience string Optional Target audience for the slide content. tone string Optional Tone of the content (professional, casual, etc.). callback_url string Optional HTTPS URL to receive async callback notification.

Note: The output will always be an image format. The exportType parameter is not accepted for this endpoint.

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
{
  "topic": "Introduction to Machine Learning",
  "language": "en",
  "domain": "presentations.ai",
  "targetAudience": "university students",
  "tone": "educational",
  "callback_url": "https://yourdomain.com/api/callback"
}

docs.title: cURL Example
docs.text:
curl -X POST https://developers.presentations.ai/api/v1/topic/singleslide \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY_HERE" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "topic": "Key Benefits of Cloud Computing",
    "language": "en",
    "domain": "presentations.ai",
    "callback_url": "https://yourdomain.com/api/callback"
  }'

docs.title: Callback URL Flow
docs.text:
If you provide a valid callback_url, the API will respond immediately with a job ID and queue status:

Immediate Response (with valid callback_url): 

{
    "job_id": "b1ed6035-1872-49d1-b773-34b920e74204",
    "status": "queued"
}

Immediate Response (with invalid callback_url): 

{
    "status": 1,
    "error": "Invalid callback_url. Must be a valid HTTPS URL"
}

When processing is complete, the result will be POSTed to your callback URL:

Callback Success Payload: 

{
    "job_id": "b1ed6035-1872-49d1-b773-34b920e74204",
    "status": 0,
    "docid": 123456,
    "docurl": "https://cdn.presentations.ai/exports/123456/single-slide-image.png"
}

Callback Error Payload: 

{
    "status": 1,
    "error": "Failed to generate single slide"
}

If you do not provide a callback_url, the result will be returned directly in the API response.

docs.title: Timeout & Polling (no callback_url)
docs.text:
• If processing takes longer than 5 minutes, the API returns immediately while continuing in the background.
  • You will receive: 
    {
  "status": 1,
  "pollUrl": "https://developers.presentations.ai/api/v1/polljob/<job_id>"
}
  • Poll the job status with GET https://developers.presentations.ai/api/v1/polljob/<job_id> (same Bearer token).
  • In-progress response: 
    {
  "status": 1,
  "message": "Document is being processed"
}
  • Completed response mirrors the success format below.

docs.title: Response Format
docs.text:
Success (200): 

{
    "status": 0,
    "docid": 123456,
    "docurl": "https://cdn.presentations.ai/exports/123456/single-slide-image.png"
}

Failure (400, 500): 

{
  "status": 1,
  "error": "Topic cannot be empty"
}

docs.title: Usage Notes
docs.text:
• Single Slide Focus: This endpoint generates exactly one slide, making it perfect for key points, summaries, or standalone content.
  • File Support: You can upload reference files to provide additional context for slide generation.
  • Target Audience: Specify your audience to get more relevant content and appropriate complexity level.
  • Tone Control: Set the tone to match your presentation style (professional, casual, educational, etc.).

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Use specific, focused topics for better single-slide content.
  • Specify targetAudience and tone for more relevant results.
  • Consider using reference files for context-rich content.

For more details about error handling, see our Error Handling Guide.
